scope.summary: Implement the positioned wind source as a first-class `WindActor` scene object (position, direction, cone angle, strength, turbulence, radius falloff) fed into `WindAnimationTracks` and uploaded to `GpuSimParams.wind` each substep, mirroring MD's positioned Wind actor
acceptance_criteria: A `WindActor` struct is defined with fields: `position: Vec3`, `direction: Vec3` (unit), `cone_half_angle_deg: f32`, `strength: f32`, `turbulence: f32`, `radius_m: f32`; it is serializable and stored in `AnimationSpec.wind_actors: Vec<WindActor>`

The WGSL `integrate.wgsl` shader computes a per-particle wind contribution by projecting the particle position into the wind actor cone and applying the falloff: particles outside `radius_m` or outside the cone receive zero wind force
`WindAnimationTracks.position` (already declared in the spec struct at line 6194) is wired to the `WindActor.position` field and keyframeable over the animation timeline

An integration test places a wind actor at a specific 3D position and asserts that cloth panels outside the actor radius are unaffected while panels within the cone deflect measurably more than with a global wind vector of equal strength
scope.risk_if_missed: Wind is only global directional; no localized wind sources are possible. Scene dynamics for animation shots (e.g. wind from a fan at a specific position, localized gust) cannot be authored. MD parity for animation wind is not achieved.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
